name: Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso
description: Cycle 46.9 miles (75.5 km) through La Mancha, visiting the iconic windmills of Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana on this difficult r
region_intro: Touring cycling around Tomelloso offers a diverse landscape for riders, situated in the heart of La Mancha. The region features a network of routes with varied terrain, including both paved surfaces and more challenging unpaved sections. Cyclists can explore the characteristic plains of La Mancha, alongside natural features such as the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park and the Peñarroya Reservoir. Elevation gains on routes can reach over 1,700 feet, providing substantial cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Tomelloso The most popular touring cycling route is Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso, a 46.9 miles (75.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers views of the iconic windmills of La Mancha. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alhambra — circular from Tomelloso, a difficult 44.5 miles (71.6 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, with some sections potentially unpaved. Local touring cyclists also love the Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso, a 38.6 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through the scenic Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Tomelloso is defined by varied landscapes, La Mancha plains, and natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Tomelloso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tomelloso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Tomelloso?
A: Tomelloso offers a range of touring cycling routes suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;3 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;20 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;12 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. Some challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057535524/alhambra-circular-from-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhambra — circular from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation gains of over 500 meters and may include unpaved sections.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Tomelloso?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;35 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Tomelloso, providing a wide variety of options for exploration. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tomelloso?
A: The terrain around Tomelloso is diverse, ranging from primarily paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels to more challenging unpaved sections. You'll cycle through the characteristic plains of La Mancha, as well as areas with notable elevation changes, particularly on routes heading towards natural parks and reservoirs.

Q: What natural features can I explore on a touring bike around Tomelloso?
A: Many routes offer access to stunning natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057518244/parque-natural-lagunas-de-rudeira-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, takes you through the scenic Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. You can also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3493285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for fishing and bathing in summer.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, several routes pass by significant landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057464109/molinos-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-y-campo-de-criptana-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; route offers views of the iconic windmills of La Mancha. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2719681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a historic Muslim fort, or explore the traditional La Mancha architecture of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posada de los Portales&lt;/a&gt; in Tomelloso's Plaza de España.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Tomelloso?
A: While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling. Summers can be quite hot in La Mancha, so early mornings or late afternoons might be preferable during those months.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tomelloso?
A: Yes, Tomelloso offers 3 easy routes that are generally more suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. The local community also hosts events like the annual 'Bike Day,' which is designed to be popular and accessible for families, starting from Plaza de España.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific parking details for each route are best checked on individual tour pages, Tomelloso is a community that embraces cycling. It's common to find parking options in and around the town center, especially near popular starting points like Plaza de España, which serves as a hub for local cycling events.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Tomelloso?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tomelloso are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.4 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from around 10 reviews. Over 200 touring cyclists have already used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Tomelloso?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Tomelloso are designed as circular loops, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057464109/molinos-de-alcazar-de-san-juan-y-campo-de-criptana-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057518244/parque-natural-lagunas-de-rudeira-circular-desde-tomelloso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Lagunas de Rudeira — circular desde Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Tomelloso has a strong local cycling culture, and you'll find local bike shops, cafes, and accommodation options within the town itself. For routes that venture further out, such as those passing through the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, it's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for points of interest or small villages where you might find refreshments.
tips: This marvelous Roman bridge is located in an environment of great scenic beauty. It is worth it to stop along the way and observe the impressive mass of stone that rises over the Záncara River.

The old ones, Sardinero, Burleta and Infante; Sovereigns of time and stories, they have been standing since the 16th century, preserving their original mechanism. The moderns, the Inca Garcilaso, the Cariari, the Vicente Huidobro, the Pilón, the Lagarto, the Culebro and the Poyatos are immense young men from 1900.

The four windmills of Alcázar de San Juan are located on the San Antón hill.

These mills are famous for the names of two of them: Dulcinea, the female character to whom Don Quixote dedicates all his exploits, and Rocinante, Don Quixote's horse.

Declared a historical-artistic monument, it is the most emblematic building
of the city, located in the Plaza de España.
Originally from the second half of the 18th century, it served as a hostel
of travelers and cavalries until the end of the 60s, being later
purchased by the City.
The Inn preserves its original structure, with its characteristic façade of
turned balusters, which resemble the corralas or corrales of comedies,
raised on a portico formed by four Tuscan columns and two
pillars. Inside, the old home and the wooden beams on the ceiling, typical of La Mancha architecture, have been preserved, currently being a Cultural Center, housing, in the same way, the Tourist Office.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 75.5 km long and generally takes around 5 hours and 12 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your cycling pace, fitness level, and time spent exploring the various points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires a very good level of fitness. The varied terrain, including unpaved sections and potential for steep gradients, makes it challenging. Some segments may even require you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Molinos de Alcázar de San Juan y Campo de Criptana touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse surface. You'll encounter approximately 34.0 km of asphalt, 15.6 km of unpaved sections, and 8.30 km of paved roads. The path types include streets, dedicated paths, and some state roads, with a small portion of singletrack. Be prepared for transitions from asphalt to dirt tracks and slight ascents.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The most striking highlights are the iconic windmills, particularly the Campo de Criptana Windmills and the Windmills of Alcázar de San Juan, famously associated with Don Quixote. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the vast Manchego plains, extensive vineyards, and pass by local hermitages and natural lagoons like Laguna del Retamar.

faqs.question: What historical and cultural landmarks will I encounter on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the windmills, which include three 16th-century mills in Campo de Criptana with original machinery, you'll pass by the Posada de los Portales in Tomelloso. The route also crosses the Bridge over the Záncara River and showcases the distinctive agricultural landscape of La Mancha, historically shaped by cereals, olives, and grapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park my vehicle when starting this route in Tomelloso?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Tomelloso serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. You should be able to find public parking within the town, especially near the Posada de los Portales or other central locations, to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given the nature of a long touring bicycle route that includes both paved and unpaved sections, and passes through towns and rural areas, it is generally not recommended for dogs. If you do bring a dog, it would need to be able to keep pace for over 75 km or be transported in a suitable trailer, and leash rules would apply in populated areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this touring bicycle route. It primarily uses public roads, dedicated paths, and unpaved tracks that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would typically be during spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers in La Mancha can be very hot, and winters can be cold, making cycling less comfortable. These shoulder seasons also offer pleasant scenery with blooming fields or harvest colors.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Tomelloso, Alcázar de San Juan, and Campo de Criptana, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. It's advisable to plan your stops in advance, especially for longer breaks or overnight stays.

faqs.question: Does this touring bicycle route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Natural de los Humedales de la Mancha (11% of the route), Camino de Campo de Criptana a Tomelloso (12%), and Camino de Alcázar de San Juan a Campo de Criptana (5%), among others.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this challenging route, pack essentials like plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes and pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. Given the varied terrain, sturdy tires are recommended. A fully charged phone and navigation device are also crucial.

waypoints.name: Windmills of Alcázar de San Juan
